memory.summary: Mina and Jae are inviting me to Echo, but my midterm tomorrow is the priority. I'm tempted to go out, yet the stress of being behind on review makes staying home feel safer.
Midterm prep finally boosted my progress, but I missed the club night with my friends. Mina's last call feels like wasted time now that my deadline looms.
Studying for the midterm finally boosted my progress, but I missed the club night with my friends. Mina's last call feels like wasted time now that my deadline looms.
Seven hours of sleep finally reset my energy, but the midterm looming still makes social plans feel like a distraction I can't afford.
Midterm is finally locked in, but I feel guilty about missing Mina's call again. My sleep debt is gone, yet social time still feels like a luxury I can't afford.
With full energy and no sleep debt, I can finally focus on the midterm. The guilt over missing Mina's call feels manageable now that my priorities are clear.
Midterm prep is done, energy's high, but I'm still avoiding Mina's call. Sleep debt cleared, yet social time feels like a luxury.
I declined Mina's request, feeling a sharp pang of guilt. My academic focus is clear now, but the social cost still stings.

desires: Complete the club social tonight without anyone noticing I'm checking my phone for exam prep instead of engaging.
Get laundry done before Sunday so I don't have to deal with the hassle tomorrow when I'm stressed about the midterm.
Feel a tiny bit less guilty about declining Mina's call, even though it stings.

goals.description: Mentally archive the midterm prep and let the 'done' feeling settle so I can stop overthinking it.
goals.rationale: Since the actual studying is already done (progress 1.0), the only thing left is the mental clutter. Clearing this immediately prevents the 'what if I forget something' anxiety from eating into my focus for the rest of the evening. It's a low-effort win that secures tomorrow without requiring social energy.

goals.description: Draft a quick, non-apologetic text to Mina declining the Echo invite.
goals.rationale: I need to address this so she doesn't think I'm ignoring her, but I don't want to sit down and have a proper conversation about it yet. Sending a short text now keeps me on track with the 'done' feeling while technically resolving the social friction without draining my current high-energy state. If I can do it in under 3 minutes, it's worth it; otherwise, I'll let it sit until Monday when I have more time to kill.

goals.description: Get laundry done before Sunday so I don't have to deal with the hassle tomorrow when I'm stressed about the midterm.
goals.rationale: It's a necessary task, but doing it right now feels like an extra step away from the club night. Since the deadline is Sunday and I'm currently in a 'soft' hardness mode, I'll defer it to avoid adding friction to my immediate gratification cycle. The guilt will linger, but the cost of interrupting the flow tonight is too high.

agent.last_reasoning: Midterm prep is done; archiving it mentally requires zero effort and prevents anxiety from eating into tomorrow's focus. Social friction with Mina is already resolved by the message. Skipping laundry until Sunday avoids friction now. Energy is high enough to recover fully tonight, so I'll sleep to reset stress before the exam.

goals.description: Decline the Echo invite politely and send Theo a quick text saying I have to study for the exam first.
goals.rationale: I need to avoid social friction right now. The group chat pressure is high, but explaining the exam as a hard constraint lets me skip the club without causing conflict or seeming like an idiot. Doing this after studying protects my peace of mind.

goals.rationale: This task has a loose Sunday deadline. Since I'm currently stressed about the exam, doing this now adds unnecessary mental load. I'll defer it until Sunday night when the urgency fades and I don't have to think about it later.
